REQ-001 (T-001), REQ-002 (T-002), REQ-003 (T-003), REQ-004/REQ-005 (T-004), REQ-006 (T-001..T-005 share), REQ-007 (T-001 share), REQ-008 (T-001..T-005 share) — every REQ-001..REQ-008 in requirements.md appears in at least one task's Requirements field (tasks.md lines 151, 405, 576, 767, 969).

AC-001..AC-035 and AC-039..AC-048 all appear in tasks.md task Requirements/Done-When AC citations (cross-checked against traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping table, which assigns each to exactly one task). AC-036/AC-037/AC-038 are the sole exceptions, correctly documented in traceability.md's 'Global Acceptance Criteria (spec-commit-bound, not implementation-phase tasks)' section as already-satisfied by this feature's own spec-phase registration commit, with a stated rationale (mirroring epic-190-a2's identical AC-034 precedent) — a valid deferral entry.
T-001 through T-005 each cite at least one REQ-NNN in their Requirements field (tasks.md lines 151, 405, 576, 767, 969). No orphan task.

T-001..T-004 titles contain 'test suite(s)' and each cites multiple AC-NNN in its Requirements field and Done When list. T-005's title contains no 'test'/'spec'/'verify' token so the ORPHAN-TEST title trigger does not apply, but T-005 also cites AC-031, AC-032, AC-033, AC-043 explicitly (tasks.md line 969-971), so no orphan condition exists regardless.

All five tasks carry Approval: Draft and Status: Planned (tasks.md lines 122/124, 373/375, 546/548, 737/739, 941/943) — satisfies both the strict round-1 rule and the more general subsequent-round rule (Approval not Approved; Status in {Planned, In Progress, Blocked}).

T-001/T-002/T-003/T-004: Risk: high -> Required Workflow: tdd (tasks.md lines 126/145, 377/399, 550/570, 741/761). T-005: Risk: medium -> Required Workflow: acceptance-first (lines 945/963). All four pairings match the risk-gate-matrix (high->tdd, medium->acceptance-first) exactly; precheck-result.json's workflow_match_precheck is PASS, consistent with this independent check.

Programmatic scan of every AC-NNN citation across all five task bodies found only AC-035 shared across T-001..T-005, and traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping explicitly differentiates it per-task ('each task's own CHANGELOG bullet + own no-version-bump grep self-check') — a distinct-scope exception, not an undifferentiated duplicate. No other AC is claimed by more than one task.
Every Blockers reference (T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004) exists in dependency-graph.json's nodes array (['T-001','T-002','T-003','T-004','T-005']) and in tasks.md itself. No dangling task-ID reference.

BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS, so this check runs. DFS over dependency-graph.json's 10 edges (T-002->T-001, T-003->T-001/T-002, T-004->T-001/T-002/T-003, T-005->T-001/T-002/T-003/T-004) found no revisited node; every edge points from a higher task number to a lower one, giving the strict topological order T-001<T-002<T-003<T-004<T-005. No cycle.

T-001..T-004 titles/scopes join 'schema/comparator' authorship with 'its test suite(s)' — test/verification work directly tied to the first clause (exception 1). T-005 bundles the vendored-copy drift-gate extension and the facet-manifest-parity suite; its own 'Note on scope breadth (TASK-SIZE)' (tasks.md lines 1025-1033) states both are REQ-006's single cross-cutting parity/discovery obligation per design.md Test Strategy item 6 ('the parity suite's own AC-032 installed-layout-discovery fixtures are the mechanism that exercises the vendored copies the --check gate protects'), not two independent feature concerns, and states no independent value exists to splitting them. No task joins two unrelated feature concerns.

Every Done When item across T-001..T-005 names a concrete test command (e.g. 'sh tests/facet-manifest-schema.tests.sh exits 0'), diagnostic-id fixture set, or artifact path (verification/T-NNN/{red,green}-sh.log, metaschema-conformance.md, vendor-check.log, MANIFEST.sha256 entries). Grep for forbidden verbs ('ensure', 'consider', 'works correctly', 'verify is correct', bare 'review'/'confirm') inside every task's Done When block found no matches (one false-positive hit on the artifact name 'Manual Review Record', not a vague instruction). No Done When item requires editing a review-frozen artifact (design.md, tasks.md body, or traceability.md); all reference CHANGELOG.md, verification/ logs, contracts/*.schema.json, or tests/* — none frozen.

traceability.md's Task Mapping/Deliverables tables list T-001..T-005 (matching tasks.md exactly) and its main table lists REQ-001..REQ-008 (all present in requirements.md). Each REQ-001..REQ-007 row cites one or more canonical <layer>-spec.md#<section> anchors (e.g. REQ-001 -> security-spec.md#trust-boundaries; REQ-003 -> security-spec.md#trust-boundaries, infra-spec.md#deployment-topology; REQ-006 -> infra-spec.md#cicd-sequence, security-spec.md#security-tests, security-spec.md#sbom-and-supply-chain), and REQ-008 uses a reasoned 'N/A — cross-layer only: <reason>' value. Every cited anchor corresponds to a real ## heading verified present in the hash-bound security-spec.md/infra-spec.md read for this round (Trust Boundaries, STRIDE Analysis, Security Tests, SBOM and Supply Chain, Deployment Topology, CI/CD Sequence, Data Residency and Retention).
